info.name: Job Platform DZ3 - End-to-End Hiring Flow
info.description: Complex Postman scenario for the job platform API: employer publishes a vacancy, applicant finds it and applies, employer updates application status.

exec: const suffix = `${Date.now()}-${Math.floor(Math.random() * 100000)}`; pm.collectionVariables.set('runSuffix', suffix); pm.collectionVariables.set('password', 'Password123!'); pm.collectionVariables.set('employerEmail', `dz3-employer-${suffix}@example.com`); pm.collectionVariables.set('applicantEmail', `dz3-applicant-${suffix}@example.com`); pm.collectionVariables.set('companyTitle', `DZ3 Hiring Company ${suffix}`); pm.collectionVariables.set('vacancyTitle', `DZ3 Backend Developer ${suffix}`); pm.collectionVariables.set('vacancyCity', 'Moscow'); pm.collectionVariables.set('resumeTitle', `DZ3 Backend Resume ${suffix}`); pm.collectionVariables.set('coverLetter', `Cover letter for DZ3 scenario ${suffix}`); ['employerToken','applicantToken','employerUserId','applicantUserId','companyId','employerProfileId','industryId','experienceLevelId','vacancyId','resumeId','viewId','favoriteId','applicationId'].forEach((key) => pm.collectionVariables.unset(key));

pm.test('201 and employer user fields', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(201); pm.response.to.be.json; const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.include.keys(['id','role','first_name','last_name','email','phone','is_verified','created_at','updated_at']); pm.expect(j.role).to.eql('EMPLOYER'); pm.expect(j.email).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('employerEmail')); pm.collectionVariables.set('employerUserId', j.id); });

pm.test('200, Bearer token and same employer', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.include.keys(['access_token','token_type','user']); pm.expect(j.token_type).to.eql('Bearer'); const expectedId = pm.collectionVariables.get('employerUserId'); if (expectedId) { pm.expect(j.user.id).to.eql(expectedId); } else { pm.collectionVariables.set('employerUserId', j.user.id); } pm.expect(j.user.role).to.eql('EMPLOYER'); pm.collectionVariables.set('employerToken', j.access_token); });

pm.test('201 and company fields', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(201); const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.include.keys(['id','title','employee_count','created_at','updated_at']); pm.expect(j.title).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('companyTitle')); pm.expect(j.employee_count).to.eql(42); pm.collectionVariables.set('companyId', j.id); });

body.raw:
{
  "title": "{{companyTitle}}",
  "description": "Company created by Postman DZ3 scenario.",
  "website": "https://dz3-example.com",
  "industry_text": "IT",
  "address": "Moscow, Postman street 3",
  "employee_count": 42
}

exec: pm.test('201 and profile links employer to company', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(201); const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.include.keys(['id','user_id','company_id','position','created_at','updated_at']); pm.expect(j.user_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('employerUserId')); pm.expect(j.company_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('companyId')); pm.collectionVariables.set('employerProfileId', j.id); });

pm.test('200 and first industry saved', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j.items).to.be.an('array').and.not.empty; pm.expect(j.items[0]).to.include.keys(['id','title','is_published']); pm.collectionVariables.set('industryId', j.items[0].id); });

pm.test('200 and first experience level saved', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j.items).to.be.an('array').and.not.empty; pm.expect(j.items[0]).to.include.keys(['id','title','is_published']); pm.collectionVariables.set('experienceLevelId', j.items[0].id); });

pm.test('201 and vacancy links company/profile/dictionaries', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(201); const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.include.keys(['id','company_id','employer_profile_id','industry_id','experience_level_id','title','salary_from','salary_to','city','employment_type','work_format','is_published']); pm.expect(j.company_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('companyId')); pm.expect(j.employer_profile_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('employerProfileId')); pm.expect(j.industry_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('industryId')); pm.expect(j.experience_level_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('experienceLevelId')); pm.expect(j.is_published).to.eql(true); pm.expect(Number(j.salary_to)).to.be.at.least(Number(j.salary_from)); pm.collectionVariables.set('vacancyId', j.id); });

body.raw:
{
  "company_id": "{{companyId}}",
  "employer_profile_id": "{{employerProfileId}}",
  "industry_id": "{{industryId}}",
  "experience_level_id": "{{experienceLevelId}}",
  "title": "{{vacancyTitle}}",
  "description": "Develop backend services for a job search platform.",
  "requirements": "TypeScript, Node.js, PostgreSQL, REST API",
  "responsibilities": "Implement API endpoints, review code and improve reliability.",
  "salary_from": 180000,
  "salary_to": 260000,
  "city": "{{vacancyCity}}",
  "employment_type": "FULL_TIME",
  "work_format": "HYBRID",
  "is_published": true
}

item.name: 08. Employer sees vacancy in own list

exec: pm.test('200 and created vacancy is in employer list', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.include.keys(['items','meta']); const found = j.items.find((x) => x.id === pm.collectionVariables.get('vacancyId')); pm.expect(found).to.exist; pm.expect(found.title).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('vacancyTitle')); });

pm.test('201 and applicant user fields', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(201); const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.include.keys(['id','role','first_name','last_name','email','phone']); pm.expect(j.role).to.eql('APPLICANT'); pm.expect(j.email).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('applicantEmail')); pm.collectionVariables.set('applicantUserId', j.id); });

pm.test('200, Bearer token and same applicant', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.include.keys(['access_token','token_type','user']); const expectedId = pm.collectionVariables.get('applicantUserId'); if (expectedId) { pm.expect(j.user.id).to.eql(expectedId); } else { pm.collectionVariables.set('applicantUserId', j.user.id); } pm.expect(j.user.role).to.eql('APPLICANT'); pm.collectionVariables.set('applicantToken', j.access_token); });

pm.test('201 and resume belongs to applicant', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(201); const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.include.keys(['id','user_id','title','desired_position','skills','experiences','salary_expectation','city','employment_type','work_format','is_published']); pm.expect(j.user_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('applicantUserId')); pm.expect(j.experiences).to.be.an('array').and.have.lengthOf.at.least(1); pm.expect(j.is_published).to.eql(true); pm.collectionVariables.set('resumeId', j.id); });

body.raw:
{
  "title": "{{resumeTitle}}",
  "desired_position": "Backend Developer",
  "about_me": "I build backend services with TypeScript and PostgreSQL.",
  "skills": "TypeScript, Node.js, PostgreSQL, Docker",
  "education": "ITMO University",
  "salary_expectation": 210000,
  "city": "Moscow",
  "employment_type": "FULL_TIME",
  "work_format": "HYBRID",
  "is_published": true,
  "experiences": [
    {
      "company_name": "DZ3 Previous Company",
      "position": "Backend Developer",
      "description": "Developed REST APIs and integrations.",
      "start_date": "2024-01-01",
      "months_count": 18
    }
  ]
}

exec: pm.test('200 and created published vacancy appears in filtered search', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.include.keys(['items','meta']); const found = j.items.find((x) => x.id === pm.collectionVariables.get('vacancyId')); pm.expect(found).to.exist; pm.expect(found.city).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('vacancyCity')); pm.expect(found.is_published).to.eql(true); });

pm.test('200 and vacancy details match created vacancy', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j.id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('vacancyId')); pm.expect(j.title).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('vacancyTitle')); pm.expect(j.company_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('companyId')); pm.expect(j.is_published).to.eql(true); });

pm.test('201 and vacancy view links applicant to vacancy', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(201); const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.include.keys(['id','user_id','vacancy_id','created_at']); pm.expect(j.user_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('applicantUserId')); pm.expect(j.vacancy_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('vacancyId')); pm.collectionVariables.set('viewId', j.id); });

pm.test('201 and favorite links applicant to vacancy', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(201); const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.include.keys(['id','user_id','vacancy_id','created_at']); pm.expect(j.user_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('applicantUserId')); pm.expect(j.vacancy_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('vacancyId')); pm.collectionVariables.set('favoriteId', j.id); });

pm.test('200 and created favorite is present', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.include.keys(['items','meta']); const found = j.items.find((x) => x.vacancy_id === pm.collectionVariables.get('vacancyId')); pm.expect(found).to.exist; pm.expect(found.id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('favoriteId')); });

pm.test('201 and application links vacancy, resume and applicant', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(201); const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.include.keys(['id','vacancy_id','resume_id','user_id','cover_letter','status','created_at','updated_at']); pm.expect(j.vacancy_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('vacancyId')); pm.expect(j.resume_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('resumeId')); pm.expect(j.user_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('applicantUserId')); pm.expect(j.status).to.eql('PENDING'); pm.collectionVariables.set('applicationId', j.id); });

pm.test('200 and applicant sees PENDING application', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.include.keys(['items','meta']); const found = j.items.find((x) => x.id === pm.collectionVariables.get('applicationId')); pm.expect(found).to.exist; pm.expect(found.status).to.eql('PENDING'); });

pm.test('200 and employer sees application for own vacancy', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.include.keys(['items','meta']); const found = j.items.find((x) => x.id === pm.collectionVariables.get('applicationId')); pm.expect(found).to.exist; pm.expect(found.vacancy_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('vacancyId')); pm.expect(found.resume_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('resumeId')); });

pm.test('200 and application status becomes INVITED', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j.id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('applicationId')); pm.expect(j.vacancy_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('vacancyId')); pm.expect(j.status).to.eql('INVITED'); });

pm.test('200 and applicant sees INVITED status', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); const j = pm.response.json(); const found = j.items.find((x) => x.id === pm.collectionVariables.get('applicationId')); pm.expect(found).to.exist; pm.expect(found.status).to.eql('INVITED'); });
pm.test('204 and empty response after favorite delete', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(204); pm.expect(pm.response.text()).to.eql(''); });

pm.test('200 and deleted favorite is absent', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); const j = pm.response.json(); const found = j.items.find((x) => x.vacancy_id === pm.collectionVariables.get('vacancyId')); pm.expect(found).to.not.exist; });
